english: kiss one's hand to; blow a kiss

etymology.description: The word 飛吻 (fēiwěn) is composed of 飛(飛) (fēi, "fly") and 吻(吻) (wěn, "kiss").
etymology.interpretation: 飛(飛) (fēi, "fly") denotes motion through the air, ideal for expressing something that travels without physical contact. 吻(吻) (wěn, "kiss") involves the lips or the act of kissing. Together, they depict a kiss that is 'sent through the air,' which naturally captures the idea of blowing a kiss or sending a kiss gesture without touch.

examples.chinese: 她向舞台上的演员送了一个飞吻(她向舞臺上的演員送了一個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiàng wǔtái shàng de yǎnyuán sòngle yí ge fēiwěn.
examples.english: She blew a kiss to the actor on the stage.

examples.chinese: 小女孩给她的妈妈送了一个飞吻(小女孩給她的媽媽送了一個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo nǚhái gěi tā de māma sòngle yí ge fēiwěn.
examples.english: The little girl blew a kiss to her mom.

examples.chinese: 在告别的时候(在告別的時候)，他用力送了个飞吻(他用力送了個飛吻)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gàobié de shíhou, tā yònglì sòngle ge fēiwěn.
examples.english: When saying goodbye, he blew a vigorous kiss.
